channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza as war grinds into the new year with no end in sight
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes killed at least 12 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, mostly women and children, officials said Wednesday, as the nearly 15-month war ground on into the new year with no end in sight.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nearly all of Puerto Rico is without power on New Year's Eve
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A blackout hit nearly all of Puerto Rico early on Tuesday as the U.S. territory prepared to celebrate New Year’s, leaving more than 1.3 million clients in the dark. Officials said it could take up to two days to restore power.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel kills Hamas commander who led heinous Oct. 7 attack on Kibbutz Nir Oz killed in drone attack: IDF
item.description: The Israel Defense Force announced that they had successfully killed one of the terrorist leaders who was responsible for the Oct. 7 attack on Israel in a targeted attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China's Xi cites global 'uncertainties' in speech ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping painted a picture of a strong Chinese economy in his New Year's speech, while also alluding to external "uncertainties" as President-elect Trump prepares to return to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Russian and Iranian groups over disinformation targeting American voters
item.description: The U.S. has imposed sanctions on two groups linked to Russian and Iranian disinformation campaigns targeting this year's election.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At least 66 people die after a truck plunges into a river in southern Ethiopia
item.description: At least 66 people were killed in southern Ethiopia after an overcrowded truck that was contracted by wedding guests plunged into a river.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu goes against doctor’s orders, appears in Israeli parliament after surgery
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appeared in the Israeli parliament on Tuesday for a crucial budget-related vote, two days after prostate surgery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montenegro extradites cryptocurrency mogul Do Kwon to the United States
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — Montenegro on Tuesday extradited a South Korean mogul known as “the cryptocurrency king” to the United States, following a decision of its justice ministry earlier this month to accept a U.S.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zimbabwe's new currency woes hit traditional stores while illegal night bazaars flourish
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — Batsirai Pabwe picked detergents, toothpaste, snacks and some pasta among several grocery items spread on the tarmac of a car parking space — an unorthodox night bazaar lit by cellphone flashlights and fluorescent lamps in Zimbabwe’s capital, Harare.
item.dc:creator: FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korea to inspect Boeing aircraft as it struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines, as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine: How the war shifted in 2024
item.description: A third year of war in Ukraine drags on as the new year and the conflict's February anniversary approach. The war in 2024 saw major shifts on the battlefield and in Western politics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean court issues warrants to detain impeached President Yoon and search his office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court issued warrants Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence over allegations of rebellion in connection with his short-lived declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US and Boeing investigators examine the site of a deadly South Korean plane crash
item.description: A team of U.S. investigators including representatives from Boeing have examined the site of a plane crash that killed 179 people in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Dozens of patients and wounded evacuated from Gaza for treatment
item.description: Dozens of patients and the wounded have been evacuated for treatment outside the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where the United Nations says Israel’s attacks on and around hospitals have pushed health care to the brink.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: From Sydney to Mumbai to Paris to Rio de Janeiro, communities around the world are welcoming 2025 with spectacular light shows, embraces and ice plunges.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Court issues arrest warrant for South Korea's President Yoon
item.description: A South Korean court issues arrest warrant for sitting President Yoon Suk Yeol over his decision in December to impose martial law over the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Serbian court convicts the parents of a teenage boy who shot dead 10 people in a school in Belgrade
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — A court in Serbia on Monday convicted the parents of a teenage boy who last year shot dead nine pupils and a school guard and wounded six more people in a school in central Belgrade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin offers to pay off debts as recruitment tool in war against Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a bill into law last month that offers to pay off up to $96,000 in debt in exchange for military service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of soldiers freed in the latest prisoner exchange between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine have exchanged prisoners of war in the latest such swap that saw the release of hundreds of captives and was brokered with the help of the United Arab Emirates, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany accuses Musk of trying to sway election
item.description: The German government on Monday accused Elon Musk of trying to influence its February election after the world’s wealthiest person penned an op-ed this weekend backing the far-right political party Alternative for Germany (AfD).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis kicks off a yearlong Jubilee that will test his stamina and Rome's patience
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis kicked off the 2025 Holy Year on Tuesday, inaugurating a celebration of the Catholic Church that is expected to draw some 32 million pilgrims to Rome in a test of the pope's stamina and the ability of the Eternal City to welcome them.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot's ex-husband won't appeal his 20-year prison sentence for orchestrating mass rapes
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The ex-husband of Gisèle Pelicot won't appeal his 20-year prison sentence for drugging and raping her and allowing dozens of other men to rape her while she was unconscious, in a case that revolted France, his lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli spy network uncovers Hezbollah commander's plans to marry his 4 mistresses
item.description: Israeli spy agencies infiltrated Hezbollah so successfully that they learned intimate details of commander Fuad Shukr's plans to marry his four mistresses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Uncovering the atrocities of the Assad regime at its 'death factory' on the hill
item.description: Sednaya prison was the site where thousands of Syrians were tortured and killed under Assad’s regime. Investigators have uncovered mass graves, and are calling for justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea orders emergency safety probe of airline operations after plane crash kills 179
item.description: South Korean acting President Choi Sang-mok ordered an emergency safety inspection of the country’s entire airline operation on Monday, a day after a fiery Jeju Air crash killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Lessons from the elections held in 70 countries in 2024
item.description: LONDON (AP) — When voters around the globe had their say in 2024, their message was often: “You’re fired.” Some 70 countries that are home to half the world’s population held elections this year, and in many incumbents were punished.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea deadly plane crash: US sends investigators to country still reeling from disaster that killed 179
item.description: The U.S. is sending investigators to South Korea to probe the deadly plane crash that killed 179 people as officials comb through over 600 body parts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean authorities seek warrant to detain impeached President Yoon in martial law probe
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean law enforcement officials requested a court warrant on Monday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate whether his short-lived martial law decree on Dec. 3 amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu to undergo major surgery after UTI diagnosis
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will undergo prostate removal surgery on Sunday amid multiple conflicts across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plane skids off runway in South Korea, killing at least 177
item.description: A passenger plane burst into flames Sunday after it skidded off a runway at a South Korean airport and slammed into a concrete fence when its front landing gear apparently failed to deploy.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia suspected in Azerbaijan Airlines crash; black boxes recovered
item.description: The U.S. officials added that if the preliminary information proves to be true, it would further underscore Russian recklessness in its invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
